query: We know customers struggle after role changes, but I cannot tell whether we need better audit logs, inline warnings, or admin approvals. Help me figure out what to build next.
why: This is ambiguous feature-delivery work inside the software-delivery family and should enter the router before a narrower skill is chosen.

query: We need stronger harness control for a long-running app project. Decide when the work should stay in one session, when to resume via compaction, and when to run a planner/generator/evaluator loop with explicit handoffs.
why: This is a software-delivery delivery-control request about cross-session orchestration, so it should route through the family router to the harness-design child.

query: After the roadmap was produced, the agent forgot the source goal during phase 1 and compaction. We need the workflow hardened so the original objective survives resume.
why: This is a harness-control problem about preserving goal lineage across phased delivery and compaction, so it should route to harness-design.

query: I already have a rollout plan for offline sync. Pressure-test the architecture, rollback plan, and observability before implementation starts.
why: This is clearly a software-delivery plan-review request and should route through the family router to the engineering review child.

query: The frontend is already built. I need an independent browser-based acceptance gate with a pass or fail verdict, evidence, and defects. Do not have the builder sign off on their own work.
why: This is a strict independent frontend signoff request, so it should route through the software-delivery family to frontend-evaluator rather than implementation or builder-side QA.

query: Build the new customer onboarding flow in our web app and use the browser to verify the responsive states before we call it done.
why: This is software feature delivery work whose dominant risk is user-facing web behavior plus builder-side browser QA.

query: We think the release is ready, but I want a confidence check on failure modes and what a serious postmortem would ask if launch goes badly.
why: This is a software-delivery readiness and reflection request that belongs in this family before routing to self-cognitive.

query: Review this vendor contract and tell me which clauses are commercially risky.
why: This is a legal review request, not software feature delivery work.

query: Explain eventual consistency in plain English with one short example.
why: This is a knowledge request, not a request to deliver a software feature through discovery, planning, implementation, or review.
